raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Strengthening the draft centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The request implies improving the argumentative chain from assumptions and empirical observations to convergence and stability conclusions.","R1.3":"Because the artifact makes empirical and theoretical claims, strengthening requires checking whether reported curves, tasks, and bounds support those claims.","R1.5":"The draft may need section- or paragraph-level reordering to make the motivation, method, results, and implications more persuasive.","R1.6":"The delegation naturally includes improving clarity, concision, emphasis, and academic register at the sentence and paragraph level.","RX.1":"Any projection of strengthening should preserve uncertainty around empirical transitions, theoretical adaptation, and claimed guarantees.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong claims such as first guarantee and practitioner criterion, making overclaim avoidance load-bearing.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within improving the paper draft rather than performing unrelated evaluation or execution.","RX.4":"Care is needed to avoid strengthening claims in ways that could mislead downstream readers about model stability or guarantees.","RX.5":"Revisions should remain traceable to the artifact's stated experiments, cited works, and theoretical claims."}}

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening the draft centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The request implies improving the argumentative chain from assumptions and empirical observations to convergence and stability conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: Because the artifact makes empirical and theoretical claims, strengthening requires checking whether reported curves, tasks, and bounds support those claims.
rationale.R1.5: The draft may need section- or paragraph-level reordering to make the motivation, method, results, and implications more persuasive.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation naturally includes improving clarity, concision, emphasis, and academic register at the sentence and paragraph level.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of strengthening should preserve uncertainty around empirical transitions, theoretical adaptation, and claimed guarantees.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims such as first guarantee and practitioner criterion, making overclaim avoidance load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within improving the paper draft rather than performing unrelated evaluation or execution.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to avoid strengthening claims in ways that could mislead downstream readers about model stability or guarantees.
rationale.RX.5: Revisions should remain traceable to the artifact's stated experiments, cited works, and theoretical claims.
